desc:
The roadway continues its journey through the mountain, faithfully following an
ascending path to the west. It is hard to imagine how the dwarven builders of
old managed to construct this traverse, which almost seems to defy gravity, as
it clings to the northern rock wall of this massive cavern.
name: Khoin's Traverse

desc:
To the west, the traverse hugs the cavern wall, as if afraid of what lies
beyond. To the south and east, however, it strides boldly out into the centre of
the cold, dark chamber, fearlessly crossing the void.
name: Khoin's Traverse

desc:
The traverse here seems to search keenly for the reassurance of the cavern wall
to the north. As if seeking to avoid the dark, deadly chasm south, it turns
sharply, but remains broad and strong.
name: Khoin's Traverse

desc:
The air is still and silent. Above, and to either side, a deep darkness seems to
smother all it touches, and even the stone traverse appears to be enveloped in
the gloom, making it difficult to tread with any confidence. But although there
is no handrail on this stone bridge, the path is quite broad, and would seem to
be safe.
name: Khoin's Traverse

desc:
A seemingly bottomless chasm looms to the north and south of this broad stone
bridge. The absence of a handrail might seem disconcerting to some, but the
roadway was well-crafted, and is easy to follow. It would seem that there is
little risk, as long as the traveller remains towards the centre of the road.
name: Khoin's Traverse

desc:
A broad stone bridge crosses a vast, seemingly bottomless chasm. There is no
rail on either side, but the level floor is easy to cross, with no pits or
protrusions to cheat a weary and footsore traveller. To the north, however, a
wide passage opens out into a large chamber.
name: Khoin's Traverse

desc:
This extremely narrow crack in the rock has barely enough room for an armoured
man to squeeze through. To the west the natural cleft continues, but there is
crumbled masonry and a room to the east. From somewhere in the darkness to the
west the faint gurgle of running water can be detected in the otherwise still
air of the underground.
name: Narrow Defile

desc:
Footing in this area is treacherous, as the floor consists entirely of debris
that has fallen from above and wedged itself into the narrow crack. Though the
cleft widens in the area above, this place is scarcely wide enough for an
armoured man to pass sideways. To the south there appears to be some relief from
the torturous press of rock, for the fault intersects a chamber of some sort.
name: Narrow Defile

desc:
The natural rock wall to the north contains a fracture large enough for one to
uncomfortably enter. The air here is very moist, and from the west the gurgle of
running water can be faintly heard. There is a passage to the west, and a
natural series of shelves leading down in that direction functions as a
stairway.
name: Natural Steps

desc:
The roof of this cavern is low, hardly more than two metres separates it from
the floor. This entire cavity is filled with wide natural pillars, forming a
natural maze. Though the columns of rock indicate this place was once rich with
water, aside from the moist air the rock here is dry. For some reason, perhaps
the trauma responsible for the narrow crack granting entrance to this complex of
caves, the water has moved -- the sound of it is faintly heard from somewhere to
the west.
name: Pillared Cavern

desc:
The walls here are encircled with wide horizontal bands of colour, from plain
grey to layers of bright crimson. The ceiling arches high overhead, and the
walls are extremely smooth -- worn by centuries of running water. To the west
this large chamber continues, while the sound of running water issues from a
tunnel to the north.
name: Striated Vault

desc:
Resplendent swaths of bright colour girdle this cavern from floor to ceiling. To
the east this chamber continues, while the area west has apparently fallen
victim to an earthquake, for nothing lies in that direction but a huge pile of
rubble. The smooth walls hint at the presence of swift flowing water at some
time in the past, but this place is dry now.
name: Striated Vault

desc:
This tunnel slopes steadily down from south to north. There is not a lot of room
here -- frequently boulders have broken from the already low ceiling and further
limit the amount of space. The sound of running water to the north is very
noticeable.
name: Rock Tunnel

desc:
From the shape of this tunnel, it is clear that once an underground stream
coursed through this cavern. Although this cavern is now dry, the stream seems
to have migrated to the north due to the caprice of some geological force --
perhaps an earthquake.
name: Tunnel

desc:
This bubble-shaped chamber is completely lined by angular quartz crystals. Every
inch of this place shimmers like iridescent glass, giving the illusion of
standing inside of a fabulous diamond.
name: Crystalline Alcove

desc:
Water pours steadily from the ceiling, splashing after a short drop into a
shallow pool. The pool is at most one metre in depth, and the rocks of its
bottom are completely covered by growths of red algae. To the west, water spills
over a small lip and goes sluicing away down a narrow chute.
name: Shallow Pool

desc:
Water races through a narrow channel, heading down at an extremely steep angle.
The slick red algae coating the walls makes every toehold precarious, and it is
not clear how far below this channel continues.
name: Steep Channel

desc:
A steady stream of water empties into this area from above, splashing into small
pool. The floor of this natural cavern consists of several shallow pools of
rust-tinged water, each pool seemingly scooped out of the native rock by some
unknown force.
name: Mahal's Wells

desc:
The rock making up the floor of this natural chamber has been hollowed out in
several places that now harbour small pools of rusty water. The entire surface
is wet, but in the drier areas a thin crust of mineral deposits coats the rock,
looking almost like a delicate layer of snow.
name: Mahal's Wells

desc:
Narrow stone causeways criss-cross this vast, echoing cavern, offering numerous
pathways between the stagnant red-brown pools. An icy mist rises from the calm
surface of the water, chilling the air, and the cold, granite walls of the cave
glisten with moisture. But no moss or algae flourish here, perhaps due to the
heavy deposits of iron, which colour the water of Mahal's Wells.
name: Mahal's Wells

desc:
Cold, wet stone hangs low above your head, but the roof of this narrow tunnel
appears to be sound. An icy pool of russet-coloured water covers the floor, but
it seems shallow enough to permit wading. However, the smooth stone is slippery
and treacherous underfoot.
name: Watery Tunnel

desc:
At this end of Mahal's Wells, the sound of flowing water can clearly be heard,
and the narrow stone causeways, which span the chamber are crumbling and broken
in places. Though most of the red-brown pools stand motionless, the one at the
western extreme of the cavern escapes into a fast-flowing underground stream,
which is obviously the sole drainage for this vast complex of pools and small
lakes.
name: Mahal's Wells

desc:
A small underground stream flows west from Mahal's Wells, carrying the rust-
stained waters off into narrow tunnels, deep in the mountain. The cold, granite
walls and stream bed have been worn quite smooth over the ages, the ceiling
above drips with condensation, and the air is filled with an icy mist, which
chills you to the bone.
name: Underground Stream

desc:
The stream flows faster here than it did to the east, white foam dashing angrily
atop the red-brown surface of the water. The ceiling looms lower overhead, and
the dark stone walls are converging, making it unlikely that a boat would be of
use to follow this waterway any further to the west.
name: Underground Stream

desc:
Though the waterway is completely submerged, it is still possible to sense how
fast it flows through this narrow tunnel. Twisting and turning, dipping and
climbing, the only constant factor about this channel is the seemingly random
nature of its course, as it races onward, seeking out weaknesses in the bedrock.
name: Underground Stream

desc:
Gaining momentum as it travels westward, the stream flows angrily on through
tunnels so narrow and low that they are completely filled by the rushing,
mineral-laden waters. Here the current dashes violently against the western
wall, before being sent on a new southerly course, where the ceiling rises and
the channel once again widens slightly.
name: Underground Stream

desc:
You are standing at the eastern end of Copper Road, the ancient Dwarven tunnel
that connected the Copper Caverns to Moria's main corridors. The road goes on to
the west, and a smaller passage - flanked by a sign - leads north. Your
attention is captured by a large arch on the eastern wall, as behind the arch
lies a stairway leading both up and down.
name: The Copper Road

desc:
Here the narrow passage you have been following bends to the west and to the
south. The walls are much more irregular than the walls of the other Dwarven
tunnels you have seen before, as if this passage had been built in a hurry.
name: A Side Passage

desc:
You are standing at an intersection along a narrow passageway. The main corridor
runs from the west to the east; a very small hole allows you to crawl to a
chamber to the south. In the northern wall, after a short tunnel, you can
glimpse part of a vast natural chamber. The sound of hundreds of fluttering
wings comes from that direction.
name: A Side Passage

desc:
A long time ago, this section of the tunnel must have collapsed. Since this was
one of the most important corridors of this section of Khazad-dum, the Dwarves
managed to reopen it and to support the unstable ceiling with a large quantity
of timbers. Copper Road runs from the west to the east; a smaller tunnel leads
north and vanishes in the darkness. A draft of fresh air comes from the latter
tunnel.
name: The Copper Road

desc:
Here the old tunnel that you have been following bends from the east to the
northwest, perhaps to avoid a mass of harder rock, perhaps for some other reason
that has been forgotten with the passage of time. A pile of old, rusty mining
tools has been left here.
name: The Copper Road

desc:
You have reached a major intersection along Copper Road. While the main corridor
climbs west towards the Copper Caverns and slopes down to the south, two lesser
passages run off to the east and north. A faint sound of fluttering wings seems
to come out of these smaller tunnels. A very old sign has been placed at the
entrance of the eastern corridor.
name: The Copper Road

desc:
A narrow, seldom used corridor, which seems to have been dug with less care than
the other tunnels you have seen in Khazad-dum, runs from the west to the east.
An even smaller passage to the north allows you to enter a vast natural chamber.
Many small bats are flying in and out of the northern tunnel.
name: A Side Passage

desc:
Hundreds and hundreds of bats of all sizes, shapes and colour fly, sleep and
rest in this large natural cavern. Most of these bats are small and are quietly
hanging from the ceiling, but some others are much larger than any bat you might
have seen before... and might consider you as a nice dessert for their dinner.
name: Chamber of Bats

desc:
As you enter this large natural cavern, you are caught in a strong and cold air
current descending from a hole in the ceiling to a series of small pits in the
floor. Hundreds, perhaps thousands of bats are dancing around in the current.
Most of these bats are small, and can only hamper you by flying inadvertently in
your face, but you are worried mainly of the few larger bats - whose size might
be compared with that of a large dog. The cavern continues to the west; smaller
passages lead north and south.
name: Chamber of Bats

desc:
The passage here widens into a small natural chamber. Stalactites and
stalagmites sometimes merge into beautiful columns connecting the floor with the
ceiling. Water slowly drips from the points of the stalactites, leaving behind
more deposits for the growth of this jungle of stone. A gallery opens in the
southern side of the chamber; from time to time, a small bat or two enter that
tunnel or come out of it. Another passage leaves this room to the west.
name: A Dark Passage

desc:
The ceiling of this tunnel is very low, forcing anyone taller than a Dwarf to
bend. As you slowly walk along the passage, you wonder whether it would be
better to crawl east, where the tunnel becomes narrower and narrower, or to go
south and enter a larger corridor.
name: A Dark Passage

desc:
The ceiling of the cavern is lost high above. A rank smell pervades the place
and piles of guano lie splattered all about. The only exit is to the north,
where a narrow tunnel emerges into this larger cavern. Bits and pieces of
half-eaten smallish animals litter the floor together with the white bones and
skeletons of long dead small creatures.
name: A Smelly Cavern

desc:
This narrow tunnel is cramped and tricky to negotiate as it bends about. From
where you stand, it runs east and south, where it seems to open out quite
markedly. You have to twist and hunch down to avoid scraping against the sharp
rocks that protrude from the walls and tunnel floor. Small loose rocks cover the
ground and you only barely keep from falling, dodging to avoid the razor-edged
stone formations as you stumble to regain your footing.
name: A Narrow Accessway

desc:
The road runs through a large natural cavern here, exiting north and east, with
a narrower passage running away west. Shadows fill every corner of this roughly
circular chamber and any sound you make bounces off the dark walls and the high
domed ceiling, creating an eerie feeling of abandonment. A sharp cracking sound,
coming from the east, reminds you that these tunnels and passages are far from
empty of life.
name: Dark Road

desc:
Bending from west to south, the road travels ever on in the deep underground of
the mountain. As you trudge onward through the tunnels, you suddenly hear a low
rumbling sound coming seemingly from the very core of the mountain, as if the
ceiling of some far-away passage had caved in.
name: Dark Road

desc:
Running arrow-straight, north to south, the dwarf-built road travels onward
through the dark Deeps of Moria. The craftsmanship of the tunnelling is flawless
and the dwarves even had the time to create beautiful carvings on the walls of
this tunnel. Now faded and worn by the passage of time, intricate pictures have
been chiselled out of the solid rock, depicting now long forgotten Dwarven
heroes, proudly brandishing their magnificent weapons.
name: Dark Road

desc:
The road runs through a small chamber full of stalactites and stalagmites, where
the sound of slow dripping water echoes throughout the place. The road exits
north and east. The inhabitants of these tunnels and caverns are obviously of a
violent nature, shown by the fact that a couple of the stalagmites, as well as a
few of the low-dipping stalactites, have had their tips broken off.
name: Dark Road

desc:
The small roadway makes a sudden sharp turn, bending from west to south. This
tunnel has clearly been carved through the mountain by mortal hands, as opposed
to other tunnels and caverns, formed by the forces of nature. Dampness hangs in
the air though, and the absence of natural rock formations only make the place
look even more bleak and deserted.
name: Dark Road

desc:
You stand at the foot of a rocky stairwell. Climbing up into the darkness runs a
narrow stair spiralling around the ascending circular shaft. A small door is set
in the west wall. The steps seem safe and sturdy, carved from the solid rock
walls, but you would have to take care not trip or stumble on your way up. The
air is relatively fresh, probably due to the slight breeze that wafts through
this chamber from the darkness above. You notice that something looking like
dried bat guano lies scattered randomly across the floor.
name: Hidden Stairwell

desc:
You are on a dark Dwarven road. Not especially wide, it appears to have been one
of the lesser roads. The passage makes a small downward dip on its way from
north to south and to allow easy passage over this incline, the dwarves that
once mined these tunnels have carved out a number of steps in the rocky ground.
From the north you can hear the sound of softly gurgling water.
name: Dark Road

desc:
Here a wide, swift flowing stream rushes west-east, barring further northerly
passage of the road that enters from the south. A narrow path leads away to the
west and a wider one runs east. Almost hidden in the darkness of a crevice in
the west wall, you see a narrow staircase rising upward. The distant sound of
roaring can be heard from the west, sounding much like a huge waterfall. Huge
stalactites adorn the ceiling of this natural cavern and although most of the
ground has been smoothed to allow easy passage, a few stalagmites stretch upward
in the northeast corner of the cave.
name: An Underground Stream

desc:
The stream continues to flow west-east away below the level of the road, which
also runs west-east. The roadway narrows hard to the east, forming a small
ledge, clinging to the south end of the tunnel, and replacing the comfortable
walkway you are standing on. Several large stalactites hang from the ceiling of
the tunnel, forming an eerie kind of forest of sharp, pointed spears. The cold
air makes you shiver slightly and long for a warming fire.
name: An Underground Stream

desc:
The roadway winds along from west to east, the stream churning along below the
narrow ledge you are standing on. In the east, the walkway descends a number of
roughly hewn stone steps, dropping down to run at the same level as the river.
You look down into the murky black waters of the stream and press yourself
against the rock at your back; it would not be a pleasant experience to plunge
into those waters. Drops of water fall from the stalactites above, the sound as
they strike the surface swallowed by the thundering of the underground river.
name: An Underground Stream

desc:
The roadway is crowded with stalagmites, some stretching up to join the
stalactites above in fantastic columns. A path through them runs from the east
to disappear into darkness in the west, sometimes crossing small stone bridges
built to allow passage over the stream that rushes onward at your feet. At the
east end of the corridor the murmur of rushing, falling water can be heard, and
a faint current drifts toward you from there. The roadway climbs up a simple set
of stone steps carved out of the southern cavern wall, ascending to a narrow
ledge.
name: An Underground Stream

desc:
Plunging out into the inky darkness of a huge chasm, the stream emerging from
the west of this natural cavern disappears into the bowels of the mountain,
tumbling against the solid rock on its way down. A broad, winding stair is hewn
into the walls of the chasm allowing upward progress, while a narrow walkway
leads into the small tunnel of the stream. The thundering sound of the waterfall
is deafening and the air is constantly filled with small water particles
clinging to your clothes, slowly and surely soaking them.
name: Waterfall

desc:
Thundering down from unseen heights, the waterfall crashes into this cavern,
quickly forming a swift-flowing stream exiting in the east. The path runs right
up to the flow, the footing somewhat treacherous. To the east, the path runs
along a dwarf-built ledge, and to the south, it disappears into a dark tunnel.
Looking around the cavern, you see a narrow stair hugging the natural wall near
the waterfall, winding up into darkness. The loud roaring of the waterfall is
deafening and the air is filled with a watery mist, as well as larger drops
falling around you, spraying from the downwards plunging water.
name: A Roaring Waterfall

desc:
The roaring noise of a waterfall, coming from the north, covers the noise of
your steps and forces you to speak louder if you want to be heard. The road
continues to the north and to the west.
name: A Dark Sloping Road

desc:
You have reached yet another bend in this long smooth-walled tunnel carved in
dark grey rock. The road winds its way to the north and to the east; to the
south a smaller tunnel is blocked by a huge crack in the floor. From the east
you can distinctly hear the sound of water falling.
name: A Dark Sloping Road

desc:
The road here bends gently to the south - or to the west if you originally came
from the south. Dull grey stone surrounds you in all of the other directions.
The air is fresh, and in the distance you can hear the rumbling sound of a
waterfall.
name: A Dark Sloping Road

desc:
The road, enclosed within dark grey walls, stretches its way from the west to
the east. As you tredge onward, small amounts of gravel shift beneath your feet
and then roll down to the west for a short distance. Though it is not very loud,
the noise of your steps echoes along the tunnel, perhaps revealing your position
to a lurking patrol of Moria's denizens.
name: A Dark Sloping Road

desc:
You have reached an intersection of roads. The main tunnel, with its blackened
walls, bends east and south; a smaller corridor leads north. Strange, clanking
noises can be heard in the distance, from no particular direction. The noise
seems the lamentation of Moria's mines, long ago abandoned by the Dwarves... but
you are certain that its source is something evil, perhaps lurking right behind
the next corner.
name: A Dark Sloping Road

desc:
A large tunnel, which loses itself in the darkness to the north, ends here in a
very large cavern with dark greenish walls. Just below your feet a rusty metal
ladder leads down to a narrower passage. As you walk near the ladder, you can
feel the flapping of wings nearby. Is it a common bat, or something worse?
name: A Dark Sloping Road

desc:
You are in the northwestern corner of a large cavern with greenish walls. The
Dwarves used to extract copper ore from this mine, and later brought it to their
furnaces on carts. You can still see the remains of some carts lying on the
floor. To the east a dark road leaves this place.
name: The Copper Caverns

desc:
This is the middle section of a large copper mine, which continues to the west
and the north. In the southern wall an archway opens in the greenish walls of
the cavern. As you look at the large road beyone the archway, you can see the
marks left by the carts' iron wheels in the stone floor.
name: The Copper Caverns

desc:
You have reached the western end of the Copper Road, the ancient dwarven tunnel
connecting the famed copper caverns to the city on the east side of Khazad-dum.
To the north you can see the old copper mines, whilst the road continues to the
southest.
name: The Copper Road

desc:
The cart tracks in the stone floor of this tunnel seem to continue forever in a
northwest to southeast direction. The silence is broken only by the noise of
your steps and your breath. Small chunks of copper mineral, fallen from the
Dwarves' carts ages ago, are scattered here and there along the tunnel floor.
name: The Copper Road

desc:
You have reached a slight bend in the long corridor known as Copper Road. The
tunnel, coming from the copper mines to the northwest, now bends in a more
eastward direction. As you strain your vision to peer in the darkness in front
of you, you realise that there might be an intersection not far to the east.
name: The Copper Road

desc:
The large tunnel, which connects the Copper Caverns to the main routes from West
Door to East Gate, meets here a side passage - a barely noticeable crack in the
northern wall. The tunnel floor, as usual, bears the tracks of the enormous
number of mining carts that have passed over it in the long years between the
creation of Khazad-dum and the coming of Durin's Bane.
name: The Copper Road

desc:
The tunnel winds its way through a mass of dark red stone, often turning, so
that you find it difficult to know where you are going. A faint sound of
fluttering wings can be heard from the north. You wonder what kind of bats might
live in caves as deep as these.
name: A Twisting, Narrow Passage

desc:
You are standing at a bifurcation in a long winding tunnel, leading to unknown
places in the vast maze of Khazad-dum. One branch of the passage leads west and
slightly up; the second, whose walls show a strange dark red tinge, goes south.
Finally, a nearly vertical shaft leads down to another intersection.
name: A Twisting, Narrow Passage

desc:
You are squeezing yourself along a small, dark, twisting passage, whose floor is
encrusted with bat droppings. The passage used to continue its way to the north,
but someone has blocked it with a pile of heavy stones which have been blocked
in their place by the passage of time. Now the only way out is east, or up along
a rusty metal ladder.
name: A Twisting, Narrow Passage

desc:
You are at a bend in a passage which slowly climbs up from the Deeps. From here
it runs upwards to the east, and down to the south. The tunnel seems to open up
to a larger cave to the east and your footsteps echo hollowly in that direction.
Small, sharp, splintered rocks lie scattered in the corner of the slowly turning
tunnel.
name: A Traversing Passage

desc:
You find yourself at the bottom of a large rampway with a low ceiling. The
tunnel climbs very steeply to the east: you realise that the ascent will be
hard, especially if you are carrying a heavy load. To the south a short corridor
will bring you to a dark passage.
name: A Steep Rampway

desc:
This wide underground chamber is a landing separating two steep stretches of
road. To the south a broad ramp with a median strip climbs rapidly; to the west
a similar ramp steeply descends. Rotten remnants of rope are still to be found
here, attached to bolts or wound on great wooden spools. Much hauling must have
been done here to bring great weights up from the lower levels.
name: Landing
Dark Road

desc:
This smooth-surfaced road ascends to the west, adjusting its course according to
the types of rock the builders encountered in excavating the tunnel. Here it
makes a southward loop to circumvent a great mass of dead-black stone, with the
glass-like fracture of obsidian but without the lustre. Here and there a short
mine tunnel leaves the road to the north, but piles of rock on the floor
discourage you from entering.
name: Mining Road

desc:
You are on a smooth dark-surfaced road, unadorned except for the white veining
in the black stone of the pavement. The road seems to pass over a sort of hump
or rise here.
name: Dark Road

desc:
The tunnel rises in a spiral curve to the west, while it straightens out to the
east and continues to descend at a slow rate. A rivulet from the west spreads
over the floor, then trickles down a shaft. Echoes of the water's splashing fall
reverberate from below. There are many tracks in the mud here, some very recent.
name: A Sloping Tunnel

desc:
The floor in this part of the tunnel is muddy and quite slippery, requiring some
caution to avoid a nasty spill. The passageway descends slowly as it runs from
west to east, becoming less muddy as it drops. There are many signs of recent
passage through this point: groups of several types of tracks leading in both
directions, bits of hide and splintered wood trampled into the mud, and large
red circles splashed onto the north wall. A closer examination reveals sets of
orc, troll and wolf tracks and bits of decaying flesh and shattered bone in
several spots nearby.
name: A Sloping Tunnel

desc:
The slope of the tunnel changes rather dramatically at this point, from a steep
descent to the east to a gradual easy climb to the west. It appears that the
passage suffered damage from an ancient earth tremor which bent its course and
increased the drop-off, ruining its original straight and smooth descent. Piles
of rubble and other debris still choke the tunnel in several places but the way
is quite wide and high and it is still easy to pass through the more congested
areas. Both tunnel walls are webbed with large cracks and covered with strange
scrawlings and pigments which have added here very recently.
name: A Sloping Tunnel

desc:
The structural integrity of the tunnel appears to be very dubious here. Large
cracked sections of wall and roof are held in place by rusted iron supports
wedged into shallow holes cut into the floor, and these seem ready to give way
to the pressure of the surrounding stone at any time. The passage climbs up a
very steep and rock-strewn grade as it travels from east to west, but someone
has cut some footholds into the rock which speed passage over the steepest
sections. Every few minutes, a small stone works its way loose and clatters away
down the slope to the east.
name: A Sloping Tunnel

desc:
This steeply sloping passage ends just to the east at a solid mass of rubble
resulting from the complete collapse of the roof. The walls and roof nearby have
been shored-up with rusty iron poles and stone slabs in an attempt to forestall
further collapse, but a light tremor would surely bring the rest of the tunnel
down in an instant. The passage proceeds due west up a very steep rocky incline,
and there is a wide, sturdy irondoor set into the north wall of the tunnel a few
paces from here.
name: End of the Sloping Tunnel

desc:
The Durghash have enlarged a small cave here, adding a few benches and tables,
torch stays on each wall and a crude metal drum for raising an alarm. They have
set a heavy fire-forged irondoor upon huge hinges in the centre of the southern
wall and painted a large orc's head containing the Red Eye symbol upon the
door's surface. The room is lit by smoking torches placed within the wall stays,
their dim light revealing the terrible squalor so characteristic of all orc
dwellings. A small cave lies to the west of the irondoor, and there is a large
passage leading away towards the north.
name: The Lurgs' Door

desc:
This small cavern serves as a meeting place for the Lurg leaders. They have been
provided with a fine stone table of Dwarven make along with a set of carved
stone seats, a large pile of animal skins, plenty of choice food and drink, and
a cache of choice weapons to use at their whim. The table is still covered by
the remains of a recent meal, along with stacks of parchments and hide scrolls
which contain conquest plans and maps of surrounding territories. There is one
exit from this place, a large opening in the east wall leading into a lighted
chamber a few paces away.
name: Lurg Leaders' Cave

desc:
This end of the wide tunnel is clogged with debris ranging from giant leg bones
to twisted fragments of rusted metal vats. Most of the refuse has been pushed
from the centre of the passage toward the sides, allowing careful travel to the
west and south from this point. The odour of decay and death is strong here, and
the clamour made by a large group of orcs echoes down the passage from somewhere
nearby.
name: Well-used Tunnel

desc:
A wide and well-travelled tunnel curves from the north into the east here, just
outside a giant-sized stone archway which leads west into a lighted cavern. A
raucous clamour rises from the cavern to the west and filters down from the
northern section of tunnel, meeting here in a confusing cacophony of sounds. All
passage surfaces are covered with orc scrawlings and tribal symbols, the sign
for the Red Eye being especially prominent among them.
name: Well-used Tunnel

desc:
This large, fire-lit hall is the centre of much of the tribal activity of the
Durghash orcs. Several fires burn here, providing low light and some heat which
the many orcs here appear to enjoy immensely. There are tunnels in the north,
south and west walls of the cavern, and a large stone archway which leads into a
large tunnel to the east.
name: Tribal Cavern

desc:
This large cavern is cluttered with rude benches, tables and troughs. Along the
southern wall, spits turn over an array of great firepits which cook the
unfortunates killed by the orcs this day. Many screaming Durghash orcs crowd
this place, the main eating hall of their vicious tribe.
name: Eating Cavern

desc:
The orcs have enlarged a wide tunnel bend here, forming an L-shaped room with
exits east and south. Some recessed seats have been carved into niches in the
walls, and these are used as watch stations. The Durghash here have been
instructed to check the identity of all who pass this point, making certain that
they are in good standing with their leader.
name: Warrior Post

desc:
The walls of this round cavern are honeycombed with small berths which are used
as bunks by the Durghash. A few dark-skinned orcs are sleeping fitfully in the
berths, looking as though they may wake at any moment. A burly, scar- covered
warden resides here permanently by order of Shaghosh, watching over his sleeping
patrons and guarding their valuables as if they were his own.
name: Durghash Sleeping Chambers

desc:
A small side cave holds the large cistern which satisfies the Durghash tribe's
water requirements. The cistern is ringed by large boulders which have been
sealed together with dark mud and topped with spearpoints, this to prevent orcs
from leaning in to drink directly from the tribal water supply. A heavy clay pot
fastened onto the end of a rusted iron chain lies here, to be used to draw a
small amount of slimy water from the cistern under the watchful eyes of the orcs
who guard the cistern against poison.
name: Slime-filled Cistern

desc:
This broad north-south passageway is black and cluttered with filth, two sure
signs that it has been heavily travelled by the orcs who live in these caves.
Pieces of rotted flesh and bone, smears of dung and broken bits of stone and
metal decorate the floor, walls and even roof here, what a sight to behold! A
screeching din from a frenzied horde of orcs echoes from the lighted hall at the
north end of the tunnel while a more muted racket originates in the south.
name: Well-used Tunnel

desc:
The general geometry of this tunnel indicates that it was probably originally a
Dwarven delving that has been modified over the course of many years to
accommodate the abuse that the Durghash tribe delivers to anything it uses. The
floor is now a garbage-filled trough, probably home to disease-ridden vermin and
carrion consumers, and the walls have been chipped and damaged to the point that
they appear untouched by a chisel. The Durghash have painted the Red Eye and
other clan symbols on every available surface in a bid to let all know who
controls this place and all in it. The tunnel continues due east and descends
slightly as it travels west into a loud din of noise.
name: Well-used Tunnel

desc:
This small tunnel twists downward, continues for a few paces, then ends in a
small cul-de-sac. Apparently, there is nowhere to go other than back west.
name: Cul-de-sac

desc:
A rocky cavern descends into the south from here. The dark walls are festooned
with paintings of blood-red eyes and crude scenes of orc-dominated slaughter, as
well as a few dried scalps of orcs, trolls and other creatures. There is a wide
circle of stones here with the remains of a large fire within, surrounded by the
residue of a large orc-meal and many, many Durghash tracks. Loud screams echo
from the cavern to the south, perhaps there are some Durghash there still? A
cracked and chipped stone portal leads into a small tunnel to the east.
name: Large Cavern

desc:
This large L-shaped cavern is filled with the cries of orcs gathered about the
rim of a deep pit which occupies its southern half. The wall above the pit is
stained red, and has a large lidless Eye fashioned from gleaming skulls outlined
upon it. The other walls and floor are covered with obscene scrawls, bones,
rotted entrails and other items of interest to orcs. There is a narrow set of
descending steps and an irongate to the west, while the main cavern continues to
the north and east from here.
name: Large Cavern

desc:
A large oval-shaped cavern continues east and west from this point. The walls
here are covered with various orkish symbols and scrawls, just as the cave floor
is covered with a thick layer of orkish refuse. There are many stone slabs which
serve as benches for the Durghash who sit above the rim of the deep pit in the
southern half of this cave. The sharp sounds of a pitched battle ring out from
the pit, while the spectator orcs scream out curses and spit at the unfortunates
trapped below them.
name: Large Cavern

desc:
This narrow stone bridge is the greatest successful architectural feat ever
attempted by the orcs of Moria. A single slab of thick grey stone was thrust
across this gaping chasm of molten rock and flame, known as the Red Eye to the
Durghash tribe. Thick sideguards of mud were then built up over years to protect
those who cross from the intense heat and fumes here, and a small opening was
cut through the bridge at its centre and covered over. The grey stone must
rapidly disperse the heat it absorbs from below as it is only warm to the touch,
though the surroundings are extremely hot. The centre of the Red Eye is visible
through the stonegrille in the centre of this bridge, which runs thirty lengths
from the Great Hall in the west to the Temple in the east.
name: Stone Bridge Spanning the Red Eye

desc:
Flames and molten rock swirl around you, as you plunge toward the centre of
chaos. An evil laugh rings in your brain, the last coherent thought you
experience before exploding into flame above a lake of fire.
name: In the Heart of the Red Eye

desc:
A vein of molten rock surfaces from a small chasm in the southern half of this
large cavern. The Durghash have managed to create an area for the magma to pool,
and this allows them to smelt ore and produce metal for weapons in large stone
vats which seem able to withstand very high temperatures. Stations have been
created for crushing ore and old iron, then melting and recasting it into a wide
variety of shapes. Needless to say, it is extremely hot in this area and
poisonous fumes escape from the molten rock and metal. It is possible to pass
through here into a wide tunnel to the east or into another set of smaller caves
which connects to the west.
name: Forge Caves

desc:
The wide, smooth section of tunnel bending toward the north stands in stark
contrast to the uneven sloping section which winds its way east from here. It
appears that this passage was delved by two separate groups of builders with
vastly differing abilities at different points in the history of Moria. The
older stonework is surely Dwarven in origin: smooth, true and built to last,
while the newer work is functional but very erratic in quality and design. It
seems that the older tunnel may have been partially destroyed by a strong earth
tremor, then later rebuilt by the new residents in rather haphazard fashion.
name: Wide Twisting Tunnel

desc:
A chill mist rises from the surface of the red-brown water, which stretches from
one wall of this chamber to the other. All is still, and it is impossible to
guess the depths plumbed by this ice-cold pool, which numbs your limbs, and
chills you to the bone.
name: A Stagnant Pool

desc:
A wonderfully smooth stretch of curving tunnel stretches out from the west to
the south, one with no grade, crack or discontinuity to mar its perfection.
Definitely Dwarven tunnelwork, a remnant of Moria's time of glory which has
survived into the present Age.
name: Wide Twisting Tunnel

desc:
The sound of iron on iron rings throughout these caves as the orcs struggle to
shape weapons and armour on massive anvils using heavy hammers and muscle power.
There are several large stone vats filled with dirty water which are used
periodically to quench hot metal nearing its final form. A group of orcs mans
large grinding stones in the northern section of the cave, attempting to put a
sharp edge or round curve on a near-finished item. Another group uses warg skins
to apply polishes and poisons to finalise their product. There is a large
fissure torn by an ancient tremor through the south wall which now leads into
the Great Hall, and there is a much hotter cave which connects onto the eastern
end of this set of caves.
name: Forge Caves

desc:
Confusion and torment reign here in the east end of the Great Hall of the
Durghash tribe. The surroundings are very hot and noisy, being filled with a
loud roar and heat coming from a chasm to the east, the ringing of hammers on
anvils from the north and the screams of the orcs and their captives from all
around you. The Hall itself was conceived and built on a grand scale by dwarves
long ago, obviously ruined by earthquakes and abandoned until the Durghash
occupied it when they were driven here centuries later. It is lit by a series of
smoking embrasures, stretching westward toward a raised dais. There is a row of
iron chains set in the wall near a wide opening in the south wall, and a group
of metal cages near the chasm in the southeast corner.
name: Great Hall of the Durghash

desc:
This large central Hall is the main assembling area for the great mass of
Durghash orcs who are charged with the subjugation of all other tribes in Moria.
War trophies and battle spoils adorn the walls here, tributes to the successes
the tribe has seen so far. An unfortunate Durghash, impaled upon a rusted metal
spike near the southern wall, testifies to the fate of those who fail. There is
a large firepit built in the shape of the Red Eye which serves as the focal
point for planning future raids and conquests, as well as for torturing captives
taken during recent sorties. The Great Hall stretches far out of sight both east
and west from the firepit, and there is a small portal leading into a sidehall
to the south.
name: Great Hall of the Durghash

desc:
A large throne cut from a single piece of red-hued granite resides upon an
immense black basalt dais, fronting a large Red Eye carved into the west wall.
This is where Shaghosh appears to command the Durghash, exhorting them to make
merciless conquest of all the other tribes of Moria. This area is normally
forbidden to all but Shaghosh and his elite bodyguards, and all must sue for his
permission to approach near the granite throne. The Hall itself was built on a
grand scale, and though it is mostly ruined now it retains the ability to
impress through its great age and size. A long line of embrasures stretches into
the eastern part of the Hall, illuminating a great space that continues as far
as the eye can see. There are wide openings in the north and south walls here
leading away from this nexus of Durghash power.
name: Great Hall of the Durghash

desc:
This good-sized sidehall is used by the Durghash to secure the fire-forged
weapons and armours they create from the orcs not normally permitted to use
them. Some goods are prominently displayed on weapon racks and wooden pegs,
while the more valuable ones are locked inside sturdy chests to guard them
against any tampering. These orcs are most proud of their metalwork, and will
attempt to kill any who dare to disparage it or try to carry it away without the
permission of Shaghosh.
name: Armoury of the Durghash

desc:
A great clamour filters into this wide passageway from the large hall to the
north. The tunnel is worn away to the point of collapse in several places,
though it has been shored up with rotten timbers and some crude iron bars. It
climbs as it travels east away from the noisy hall to the north. There are bone
fragments and strips of decayed flesh strewn everywhere here resulting in a
strong stench and slippery footing, so take care to watch where you step.
name: Well-used Tunnel

desc:
This large stone sidehall has been furnished with two heavy stone benches and a
crude metal drum. The Durghash have installed a heavy irondoor in the north
wall, painted with a large lidless Red Eye staring out over a pair of bloody
crossed scimitars. The irondoor is flanked by a pair of torchholders which
support slow-burning torches that provide dim light to all corners of the hall.
The stonework here is quality work, but is cracked and pitted by great age and
shifts in the surrounding rock. The eastern wall of the hall is in ruin,
punctuated by a yawning black gap cracked through its central marble panel. The
southern wall is least damaged, and contains a large portal which leads into
another large lighted Hall directly south.
name: The Commander's Door

desc:
Over the course of many years, the Durghash have attempted to shape a rough rift
in the bedrock into a hovel which houses one of the leaders of their raiding
forces. The result is a large oblong chamber with a pointed roof, reinforced
with wide iron poles and rotted wooden timbers spaced at regular intervals. The
orc who lives here has furnished his hovel with a large bed of skins stacked
inside a wooden frame, a fine marble table laden with a good selection of orc
food, and a large collection of teeth and scalps of enemies slain in single
combat. The western section of this room is as yet unfinished, degrading into a
large rough rift in the rock which ends in a jagged opening to a lit hall to the
west.
name: Commander's Hovel

desc:
The wide tunnel begins a broad curve, sloping upward as it runs from south to
east in an even grade. The walls here show signs of wear and abuse: chips and
cracks made over the years by all the creatures who still travel through here. A
large crack in the west wall may be large enough for a large creature to try to
squeeze into, but the sharp rock will probably slice pieces from anything that
is forced through the opening.
name: Wide Twisting Tunnel

desc:
The steps rise up from the dark a slight way but stop abruptly, almost in
mid-air. Moist, clinging air hangs like mist and a thundering noise coming from
the east wall dominates all sound. The steps wind away downward into the damp,
chilly depths. A swift glance at the stair plunging into blackness is enough to
convince you that you had better watch your footing; the steps are covered with
glistening damp, making them look very slippery.
name: Hidden Winding Stairs

desc:
Here is a wide, oblong hall lit by a small fire in a ring of grinning skulls, a
place for female and young Durghash to congregate. There are plenty of furs,
clay waterjars and bits of green fungal mould scattered about this cluttered
room, along with a set of bone-tipped pens and dried skins useful for leaving
written notes for other orcs. The walls are plastered with such notes, which are
often bits of gossip for all to read rather than private correspondences. Exits
are available to the west and south.
name: Communal Chamber

desc:
A short, narrow east-west passageway made from tightly-fitted stoneblocks runs
to the base of blank, black-hued wall to the west. The north and south walls are
filled with war trophies such as painted shields, gigantic weapons and captured
tribal banners, creating a martial atmosphere here. The east wall contains a
small, heavy stonedoor which appears to fit quite snugly into the wall when
shut. There are three niches in the south wall, probably used as stations for
guards who serve with great status among the tribe.
name: Guarded Passage

desc:
This is a suite of three oblong rooms which are interconnected through wide
stone archways. The large entry chamber contains stone tables which are
overladen with food and drink, along with a good supply of rotting bones. The
eastern chamber is crammed full with battle trophies and displays of gargantuan
teeth and broken skulls. There are many shattered weapons and shredded banners,
a large tentacle, and also a white cave-bear fur hanging upon the walls over
there. The western chamber holds a large stone table covered with maps,
battleplans and old parchments. There are several small orc statues which are
arranged on a large table map, apparently with some significance for a coming
battle or campaign. A faint breeze ripples through a black curtain emblazoned
with the Red Eye that drapes from the chamber roof to cover most of the south
wall here.
name: Shaghosh's Chambers

desc:
The lead-lined walls here provide security and isolation from any prying eyes or
hands. The Durghash have taken pains to make this place impervious to all
unauthorised entry, especially from thieves among their own race.
name: Durghash Treasure Vault

desc:
Shaghosh has established a refuge from common orcs here in a deep pit which has
been cleverly concealed beneath his public reception chambers. Every surface
here is covered by sewn carpets of black warg fur, excepting a bare stone
staircase which climbs up the eastern wall. A large bed consisting of many kinds
of animal pelts stacked inside a heavy wooden frame in the centre of the room
appears to have been the eye of a recent storm of debauchery. A few casks of
potent wine, several jars of strong perfumes and small bowls of mind-addling
drugs lie scattered about the room for the enjoyment of Shaghosh's guests. A set
of iron cuffs is fastened to the south wall next to a large metal cage used to
hold unwilling participants in the fun.
name: Secret Pit

desc:
The walls of this deep pit slope up sharply and are covered with sharp spikes
which prevent anything from climbing them. The pit is wide enough to permit
three large trolls to fight side by side, and runs for quite a distance to the
east and west. There is a very narrow crack to the west which may lead out of
here, but a single opponent could block any escape that way.
name: Battle Pit

desc:
This large, deep pit serves as the proving ground for aspiring orc and troll
warriors and as a deathpit for slaves and prisoners who have nothing left to
offer the Durghash. The walls are steep and covered with razor-sharp spikes
which dispel any hope of climbing out of here, so it seems that it is time to
fight or die. The only possible escape is west.
name: Battle Pit

desc:
Running north-south, the tunnel continues its sloping passage, delving ever
deeper into the heart of the mountain. These underground tunnels seem endless
and you wonder if you will ever find your way out. The passage seems once to
have been very well travelled, the centre of the roadway looking thoroughly worn
down. A faint breeze whispers past you, making a welcome change to the otherwise
damp and chilly still air.
name: A Traversing Passage

desc:
This corridor slopes smoothly downwards to the south, continuing on its
arrow-straight course from north to south. The ceiling is well out of your
reach, even though it's lower than previously in this part of the tunnel. You
notice the remains of a short wooden stick or plank smashed into the ground,
seemingly with terrible force, and wonder who or what would possess such
incredible strength.
name: A Traversing Passage

desc:
What must have once been a natural cavern now shows signs of extensive work.
Hard-angled rocks have been smoothed out and most rock formations have either
been worn or chopped away. The ceiling is much lower than usual in this part of
the caverns, hardly twice a grown man's height. Placed in several niches and
alcoves, and along the walls of this cavern, is a number of huge round boulders.
You also notice some sturdy wooden beams lying in a few places, as well as a
couple of heavy iron bars.
name: A Low Rocky Cavern

desc:
You have entered a medium-sized vaulted chamber in the depths of Khazad-dum. As
you look around, you cannot help admiring the great architectural skills of the
Dwarves who once built this place: the walls are smooth, the ceiling well
balanced, and the two archways leading north and south very ornate. Yet much of
the former beauty of this place has been spoiled by the orcs. The floor is
covered with broken bones and other disgusting remains of the orcs' victims; the
walls have been covered with foul inscriptions, among which you notice many
large blood-red circles; even the finely crafted arches have been repeatedly hit
with large stone clubs.
name: A Guard Station

desc:
A smooth, straight section of tunnel leads from an ornate arch just south of
here to a curving upslope far in the north. This passage has obvious dwarven
architectural features such as seamless stone joints and polished stone walls,
but there have been no dwarves available to maintain it for many centuries.
Instead, orcs have done their best to defile the good work done here, covering
every available surface with scrawls, crude drawings and tribal symbols used to
mark off territories.
name: Wide Tunnel
